video.title: Are Fitness Trackers Actually Accurate?
video.description:
This is Part 5 from the full Speediance/Fitness Tech workout discussion. I talk about fitness-tracker accuracy, why "not perfect" does not mean useless, and how Garmin Body Battery and Whoop recovery can measure different things while still providing useful signal.

Then I tell the story of a Whoop reading that looked broken, showed a half-marathon worth of strain in my sleep, and eventually helped point me toward a real AFib emergency.

Full episode: https://youtu.be/GzqAzoSKBWQ

Not medical advice. Fitness trackers are not a diagnosis. This is personal experience and commentary; talk to qualified medical professionals for heart-rate, AFib, training, or health concerns.

Chapters:
00:00 Are fitness trackers accurate?  
01:30 Garmin Body Battery while sick  
03:00 Recovery score vs body battery  
04:30 Why not-perfect does not mean useless  
05:44 The Whoop half-marathon-in-my-sleep warning  
07:30 A 160 bpm strength-training moment  
09:00 Doctor call and hospital visit  
10:30 Stress, training, and the diagnosis  
12:00 Jiu-jitsu tangent and training choices  
13:30 Training while sick and doctor questions

#Garmin #Whoop #FitnessTrackers
